hypothesis: Within the stated one-step scalar block-density/flux ansatz, Rule 30 has no solution beyond constants and certified spatial coboundaries for density widths 1 through 5 over either the rationals or GF(2).
interpretation: Every equation in all ten bounded systems was solved exactly. In each case the full nullity equals the independently constructed trivial-subspace rank, so this ansatz has zero excess dimension through width 5. This finite negative search does not rule out wider, nonlocal, nonlinear, differently valued, or otherwise structured identities.
limitations: Density width is bounded by five and flux width by six.
Only one-step scalar block observables with nearest-boundary telescoping flux are included.
Only rational and GF(2) coefficient fields are tested.
Zero excess at tested widths does not prove global nonexistence.
Even a spatial conservation law would require a separate argument to bound center-column discrepancy.

parameters.positive_control: Rule 204 at width 1 is detected by the unit tests
parameters.tested_identity: rho(F_k(w))-rho(w[1:-1]) = J(w[:-1])-J(w[1:]) for every length-(k+2) binary word

proof_scope: Exact ranks and nullities of ten explicitly defined finite linear systems, plus exact verification and rank of their constructed trivial solution subspaces.
